description_long: This chair features a geometric, angular design constructed entirely from light-colored wood, reminiscent of the De Stijl movement and the iconic Red and Blue Chair by Gerrit Rietveld. The seat and backrest are made from flat, rectangular wooden panels, while the frame consists of straight, squared wooden beams joined at right angles. The chair includes wide, flat armrests and a slightly reclined backrest for ergonomic support. The visible wood grain and knots add a natural, unfinished aesthetic, and the open frame structure gives it a lightweight, airy appearance. Its robust construction and minimalist style make it suitable for both modern urban parks and contemporary indoor spaces.
description: A minimalist, angular wooden chair with a flat seat, reclined backrest, and wide armrests, showcasing visible wood grain and a sturdy open-frame design.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the tall, flat backrest panel and the squared wooden frame supporting it.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the reclined backrest, flat seat, and the angular armrest structure.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the wide seat, parallel armrests, and the open frame beneath the seat.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, emphasizing the chair's angular geometry and structural beams.
